expected_behavior: 2 entities x 2 time periods x 2 metrics = 8 sub-questions. Aggregator computes ratio from cited figures.
question: Compute and compare R&D as a percentage of revenue for Apple and Meta in both FY2023 and FY2024 based on their 10-K filings.
ground_truth: Apple: FY2023 R&D ~$29.9B / revenue ~$383.3B = ~7.8%. FY2024 R&D ~$31.4B / revenue ~$391.0B = ~8.0%. Meta: FY2023 R&D ~$38.5B / revenue ~$134.9B = ~28.5%. FY2024 R&D ~$43.6B / revenue ~$164.5B = ~26.5%. Meta invests proportionally 3x more in R&D than Apple. All figures must cite respective [SEC-*] 10-K chunks.
key_facts: Must cite both years for both companies.
Answer should compute/show the percentages explicitly.
Should note Meta invests proportionally much more in R&D.

pipeline.answer:
# R&D as a Percentage of Revenue: Apple vs. Meta (FY2023 & FY2024)

## Overview

This analysis compares Apple Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc. R&D spending relative to revenue for fiscal years 2023 and 2024, based on their respective 10-K filings. The two companies have markedly different R&D intensity profiles, reflecting their distinct business models and strategic priorities.

---

## Apple — R&D & Revenue Data

### Revenue (Total Net Sales)

| Fiscal Year | Revenue (Billions USD) | Year-over-Year Change |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **FY2023** | $383.3 | — |
| **FY2024** | $391.0 | +2% |

*Source: Apple 10-K for FY2024 [1][3]*

### R&D Expense

| Fiscal Year | R&D Expense (Billions USD) | Year-over-Year Change |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **FY2023** | $29.92 | — |
| **FY2024** | $31.37 | +5% |

*Source: Apple 10-K [3]*

### R&D as Percentage of Revenue

| Fiscal Year | R&D % of Revenue | Change vs. Prior Year |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **FY2023** | 7.8% | — |
| **FY2024** | 8.0% | +0.2 pp |

R&D as a percentage of revenue held steady at **8%** in both years (rounded), up from 7% in FY2022 [3].

### Key Investment Areas

Apple's R&D investments span **semiconductor design (Apple Silicon), software development, machine learning, augmented reality (Apple Vision Pro), health monitoring technologies, and Apple Intelligence** [1]. Growth in FY2024 was driven primarily by increases in headcount-related expenses [3].

---

## Meta Platforms — R&D & Revenue Data

### Revenue (Total)

| Fiscal Year | Revenue (Billions USD) | Year-over-Year Change |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **FY2023** | ~$134.9 | — |
| **FY2024** | $164.5 | +22% |

*Source: Meta 10-K and earnings context [2][3]*

### R&D Expense

| Fiscal Year | R&D Expense (Billions USD) | Year-over-Year Change |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **FY2023** | $38.483 | +8.9% from 2022 |
| **FY2024** | $43.873 | +14.01% from 2023 |

*Source: Meta 10-K [3]*

### R&D as Percentage of Revenue

| Fiscal Year | R&D % of Revenue | Change vs. Prior Year |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **FY2023** | 28.5% | — |
| **FY2024** | 26.7% | -1.8 pp |

The decline from 28.5% to 26.7% indicates that revenue grew at a faster pace (+22%) than R&D spending (+14%) during FY2024 [2][3].

### Key Investment Areas

Meta's R&D is heavily focused on **artificial intelligence, the metaverse (Reality Labs), and infrastructure**. In 2023, 80% of Meta's expenses were attributed to cost of revenue and R&D combined [1]. The "Other Revenue" category (WhatsApp Business Platform, Meta Verified) has been doubling each year, indicating new monetization avenues [2].

---

## Side-by-Side Comparison Table

| Metric | Apple FY2023 | Apple FY2024 | Meta FY2023 | Meta FY2024 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Revenue (Billions USD)** | $383.3 | $391.0 | ~$134.9 | $164.5 |
| **R&D Expense (Billions USD)** | $29.92 | $31.37 | $38.483 | $43.873 |
| **R&D % of Revenue** | **7.8%** | **8.0%** | **28.5%** | **26.7%** |
| **Revenue Growth (YoY)** | — | +2% | — | +22% |
| **R&D Growth (YoY)** | — | +5% | — | +14% |

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **Meta invests 3–4x more of its revenue in R&D** than Apple. In FY2024, Meta's R&D intensity was 26.7% vs. Apple's 8.0%, reflecting Meta's aggressive bet on AI and the metaverse.

2. **Absolute R&D spending:** Despite much lower revenue, Meta outspends Apple on R&D in absolute terms — $43.9B vs. $31.4B in FY2024.

3. **Divergent trends:** Apple's R&D as a percentage of revenue was **stable or slightly increasing** (8% in both years), while Meta's **declined** from 28.5% to 26.7% because revenue growth (+22%) outpaced R&D growth (+14%).

4. **Strategic contrast:** Apple maintains a leaner R&D profile, focusing on targeted hardware-software integration (Apple Silicon, Vision Pro). Meta, by contrast, is in a high-investment phase across AI, VR/AR, and infrastructure, reflected in its consistently high R&D ratio.

urls.title: Apple's R&D spending climbs to 10% of revenue on AI investments
urls.snippet: [Skip Navigation](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/06/apples-rd-spending-climbs-to-10percent-of-revenue-on-ai-investments.html#MainContent). [Markets](https://www.cnbc.com/markets/). [Create free account](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/06/apples-rd-spending-climbs-to-10percent-of-revenue-on-ai-investments.html#). [Markets](https://www.cnbc.com/markets/). [Business](https://www.cnbc.com/business/). [Investing](https://www.cnbc.com/investing/). [Tech](https://www.cnbc.com/technology/). [Video](https://www.cnbc.com/tv/). [Tech](https://www.cnbc.com/technology/). Apple’s R&D spending hit 10.3% of revenue in the [March quarte](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/30/apple-aapl-q2-2026-earnings-report.html)r, up from 7.6% in the prior period and 9% in the same quarter a year earlier. Gene Munster, managing partner at Deepwater Asset Management, said Apple’s push puts it closer to the hyperscalers, noting that across [Google](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/GOOGL/), [Microsoft](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/MSFT/), [Meta](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/META/) and [Amazon](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/AMZN/), the average year-over-year R&D increase for the quarter was 29%. But the R&D increase got some attention from Cook, who will be turning over the top job to longtime hardware boss [John Ternus](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/20/apple-new-ceo-john-ternus-faces-defining-challenge-fixing-ai-strategy.html) in September. ## [](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/06/apples-rd-spending-climbs-to-10percent-of-revenue-on-ai-investments.html)Capex gap remains. The company is leaning heavily on Google, [announcing](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/12/apple-google-ai-siri-gemini.html) in January that the search giant’s Gemini technology would power its AI features, including the forthcoming Siri upgrade. *   [](https://www.facebook.com/cnbc/). *   [](https://www.twitter.com/cnbc/). *   [](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cnbc/). *   [](https://www.instagram.com/cnbc/). *   [About CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/about/). *   ![Image 20: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Opt-Out Icon](https://static-redesign.cnbcfm.com/dist/17269f1b6083fd5f61be.svg)[Your Privacy Choices](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/06/apples-rd-spending-climbs-to-10percent-of-revenue-on-ai-investments.html).

urls.title: Apple 10-K Analysis 2024: Annual Report Deep - Libertify.com
urls.snippet: # Apple 10-K Analysis 2024: Comprehensive Annual Report Breakdown. ## Apple 10-K Analysis: Fiscal 2024 Financial Overview. The **Apple 10-K analysis** for fiscal year 2024 reveals a company that continues to demonstrate remarkable financial resilience while navigating an increasingly complex global landscape. Apple Inc. reported total net sales of $391 billion for the fiscal year ended September 28, 2024, representing a 2% increase from $383.3 billion in fiscal 2023. While this growth rate appears modest by historical standards, it masks significant underlying dynamics including the acceleration of high-margin Services revenue and the strategic positioning of Apple’s product ecosystem for the AI era. The **Apple 10-K analysis** of revenue composition reveals the continued centrality of iPhone to Apple’s business model. Perhaps the most significant finding in any **Apple 10-K analysis** is the continued acceleration of the Services segment, which generated $96.2 billion in fiscal 2024 — a 13% year-over-year increase.

urls.snippet: Members of the Board of Directors, executive officers and any other individuals subject to Section 16 (the “Section 16 reporting persons”) of the Act must also comply with the reporting obligations and limitations on short-swing profit transactions set forth in that section of the Act. Rule 10b5-1 Trading Plans The trading restrictions set forth in this policy do not apply to transactions under a Trading Plan that: • has been submitted to and pre-approved by Apple’s Corporate Legal team; • includes a “Cooling Off Period” for; o Section 16 reporting persons (in which case the Cooling Off Period extends to the later of 90 days after adoption or modification of a Trading Plan or two business days after filing the Form 10-K or Form 10-Q covering the fiscal quarter in which the Trading Plan was adopted, up to a maximum of 120 days); and o all other employees and other covered persons or entities, other than the company (in which case the Cooling Off Period extends to the later of 30 calendar days after the date the plan is executed and 24 hours after the public announcement of our earnings for the quarter in which the plan is executed).

urls.title: Document
urls.snippet: Meta Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results. (Nasdaq: META) today reported financial results for the quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Highlights. Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Operational and Other Financial Highlights. •Revenue – Revenue was $48.39 billion and $164.50 billion, representing increases of 21% and 22% year-over-year for the fourth quarter and full year 2024, respectively. Revenue on a constant currency basis would have increased 21% and 23% year-over-year for the fourth quarter and full year 2024, respectively. •Costs and expenses – Total costs and expenses were $25.02 billion and $95.12 billion, representing increases of 5% and 8% year-over-year for the fourth quarter and full year 2024, respectively. Free cash flow was $13.15 billion and $52.10 billion for the fourth quarter and full year 2024, respectively. We expect first quarter 2025 total revenue to be in the range of $39.5-41.8 billion.

urls.title: Meta Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
urls.snippet: ### Meta Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results. (Nasdaq: META) today reported financial results for the quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. **Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024** **Financial Highlights**. **Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024** **Operational and Other Financial Highlights**. We expect first quarter 2025 total revenue to be in the range of $39.5-41.8 billion. This reflects 8-15% year-over-year growth, or 11-18% growth on a constant currency basis as our guidance assumes foreign currency is an approximately 3% headwind to year-over-year total revenue growth, based on current exchange rates. We expect full year 2025 total expenses to be in the range of $114-119 billion. Based on the servers and network assets placed in service as of December 31, 2024, we expect this change in accounting estimate will reduce our full year 2025 depreciation expense by approximately $2.9 billion.

chunks.title: https://www.libertify.com/interactive-library/apple-10k-annual-report-2024/
chunks.chunk_text:
t reported results. The company uses derivative instruments to partially hedge these exposures, but the complex regulatory environment for financial instruments adds layers of compliance requirements. Competition risk spans all product categories, with the 10-K specifically noting competitive pressures in smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and digital services.
Research and Development: Innovation Investment in the Apple 10-K
The Apple 10-K analysis of research and development expenditures reveals Apple’s commitment to sustained innovation. R&D spending has consistently increased as a percentage of revenue, reflecting the company’s investment in next-generation products and technologies including Apple Silicon, Apple Intelligence, Apple Vision Pro, and health technology features. Selling, general and administrative expenses increased by $1.2 billion during fiscal 2024 compared to 2023, partly driven by expanded operations and workforce growth.
The company’s R&D investments span multiple technology domains, including semiconductor design, software development, machine learning, augmented reality, and health monitoring technologies.

chunks.chunk_text:
lysis: Balance Sheet and Capital Allocation — Apple’s balance sheet as disclosed in the 10-K filing reflects extraordinary financial strength.
- Regional Performance: Geographic Analysis from Apple’s 10-K — The geographic breakdown in the Apple 10-K analysis reveals important patterns in Apple’s global revenue distribution.
Apple 10-K Analysis: Fiscal 2024 Financial Overview
The Apple 10-K analysis for fiscal year 2024 reveals a company that continues to demonstrate remarkable financial resilience while navigating an increasingly complex global landscape. Apple Inc. reported total net sales of $391 billion for the fiscal year ended September 28, 2024, representing a 2% increase from $383.3 billion in fiscal 2023. While this growth rate appears modest by historical standards, it masks significant underlying dynamics including the acceleration of high-margin Services revenue and the strategic positioning of Apple’s product ecosystem for the AI era.
The filing, submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission, provides investors with a comprehensive view of Apple’s financial health, strategic priorities, and risk factors.

Services gross margin increased during 2024 compared to 2023 due primarily to higher Services net sales. The Company’s future gross margins can be impacted by a variety of factors, as discussed in Part I, Item 1A of this Form 10-K under the heading “Risk Factors.” As a result, the Company believes, in general, gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure.

Total net sales include $7.7 billion of revenue recognized in 2024 that was included in deferred revenue as of September 30, 2023, $8.2 billion of revenue recognized in 2023 that was included in deferred revenue as of September 24, 2022, and $7.5 billion of revenue recognized in 2022 that was included in deferred revenue as of September 25, 2021.

The Company’s proportion of net sales by disaggregated revenue source was generally consistent for each reportable segment in Note 13, “Segment Information and Geographic Data” for 2024, 2023 and 2022, except in Greater China, where iPhone revenue represented a moderately higher proportion of net sales. As of September 28, 2024 and September 30, 2023, the Company had total deferred revenue of $12.8 billion and $12.1 billion, respectively. As of September 28, 2024, the Company expects 64% of total deferred revenue to be realized in less than a year, 25% within one-to-two years, 9% within two-to-three years and 2% in greater than three years.

🔑 Key Takeaways
- Apple 10-K Analysis: Fiscal 2024 Financial Overview — The Apple 10-K analysis for fiscal year 2024 reveals a company that continues to demonstrate remarkable financial resilience while navigating an increasingly complex global landscape.
- Revenue Breakdown: iPhone Dominance and Apple 10-K Insights — The Apple 10-K analysis of revenue composition reveals the continued centrality of iPhone to Apple’s business model.
- Services Segment: The Growth Engine in Apple’s Annual Report — Perhaps the most significant finding in any Apple 10-K analysis is the continued acceleration of the Services segment, which generated $96.
- Apple 10-K Analysis: Balance Sheet and Capital Allocation — Apple’s balance sheet as disclosed in the 10-K filing reflects extraordinary financial strength.
- Regional Performance: Geographic Analysis from Apple’s

chunks.title: https://www.libertify.com/interactive-library/apple-10k-annual-report-2024/
chunks.chunk_text:
ndamentally reshape the tax landscape for multinational technology companies, potentially increasing Apple’s effective tax rate and reducing the advantages of intellectual property holding structures. For investors conducting an Apple 10-K analysis, understanding the evolving tax landscape is essential for accurate earnings projections.
📊 Explore this analysis with interactive data visualizations
Frequently Asked Questions
What was Apple’s total revenue in fiscal 2024?
Apple reported total net sales of $391 billion for fiscal year 2024, representing a 2% increase from $383.3 billion in fiscal 2023, driven primarily by growth in Services revenue and iPhone sales.
How much did Apple’s Services segment generate in 2024?
Apple’s Services segment generated $96.2 billion in fiscal 2024, representing a 13% year-over-year increase and accounting for approximately 24.6% of total company revenue, making it the fastest-growing segment.
What are the key risks identified in Apple’s 10-K filing?
Key risks include global economic uncertainty, supply chain concentration in Asia, regulatory and legal challenges including the EU State Aid Decision ($10.2 billion tax charge), competitive pressures in all product categories, and currency fluctuation impacts on international operations.
How much did Apple spend on share buybacks in 2024?
Apple authorized up to $110 billion in share repurchases under its May 2024 program, with $20.9 billion utilized by September 2024.

chunks.title: s25_META.pdf
chunks.heading: Other Revenue
chunks.chunk_text:
Other Revenue

The smaller portion of Meta’s Family of Apps revenue is

the other revenue category which includes revenues from

WhatsApp Business Platform and Meta Verified

subscriptions. This category seems insignificant to Meta’s

total revenue, but it has been growing at a substantial rate

with it doubling in growth each of the past two years.

WhatsApp Business Platform allows businesses to

communicate with customers directly , create

advertisements, showcase products and services, and

create automatic responses for companies at a larger

scale 21 . Meta Verified subscriptions allow user s of

Facebook and Instagram to inte ract with additional

features after paying for a monthly subscription. As stated

previously, some of these features consis t of a verification

badge, additional account security protection, and faster

customer service with each app. With Meta’s large AI

investment, we believe they will continue to add or

enhance additional functions for both WhatsApp Business

Platform and Meta Verified subscriptions. This will lead to

a high retention rate for cu rrent businesses and users and

continued high growth over the next 5 years. We forecast

that other revenues will grow at a CAGR of 18.8% through

2029 which exceeds consensus estimates five -year CAGR

of 14.4%.

> Source: Meta 10 -K& HF Estimates

chunks.title: s25_META.pdf
chunks.heading: Regional Breakdown
chunks.chunk_text:
Regional Breakdown

Revenue & Average Price Per Ad (APPA) :

Of the two segments Meta operates in, they break down

their global revenue and year -over -year percentage

change in average price per ad (APPA) into four regions.

These four regions are the United States and Canada,

Europe, Asia -Pacific, and the Rest of World. The sum of

these four regions produced Meta a total revenue of

$164.50 billion for the year 2024. Of that amount, $63.21

billion was from the Un ited States and Canada, $38.36

billion was from Europe, $45.01 billion was from Asia -

Pacific, and $17.92 billion was from the Rest of World 11 .

> Source: Meta 10 -K

As stated before, Meta also reports each region’s year -

over -year average price per ad (APPA) to show how prices

are trending a cross the globe. This metric is calculated by

taking the total advertising revenue per region and

dividing it by the number of ads delivered per region.

Although this does not account for Meta’s total revenue,

it is an important metric to follow as advertising revenue

makes up almost all of their revenue. Meta’s worldwide

average price per ad (APPA) increased 10% year -over -year

which was driven by an increase in advertising demand but

was slightl y offset by increased ad deliveri es 11 .

> Source: Meta 10 -K

China Revenue Growth:

With the ongoing feud between the U.S. and China in the

tariff war, Meta faces the risk of losing a high -growth

segment of its revenue. China does not make up a large

chunks.title: s25_META.pdf
chunks.heading: Recent Earnings Announcement
chunks.chunk_text:
Recent Earnings Announcement

when calculating depreciation and amortization, and

property and equipment.

The last piece of guidance Meta provided was that their

total cost and expenses for 2025 would fall in the range of

$114 billion - $119 billion 4. The approximate increase of

$20 billion was said to be expected because of increased

infrastructure costs and an increase in headcount at

Meta 4. After adjusting for the guidance they provided, our

total cost and expense for 2025 came out to be $114.5

billion which is in line with the low end of their guidance.

Other than the two guidance pieces mentioned, Meta

ann ounced they would not be giving revenue guidance for

2025.

chunks.title: s25_META.pdf
chunks.heading: Recent Earnings Announcement
chunks.chunk_text:
Recent Earnings Announcement

On January 29, 2025, Meta reported their Q4 earnings and

impressed investors with their results and initiatives for

2025. The expectation for Q4 revenue was $47.0 billion

and earnings per share was $6.77 3. The actual results came

in at $48.4 billion for revenue and $8.02 for earnings per

share 3. Meta posted an impressive double -beat at a time

when the stock market was already performing very well.

The strong growth in revenue and earnings was driven by

a 14% increase in average price per ad (APPA) 11 .

Additionally, their total performance for 2024 was driven

by an 11% increase in ad impressions and a 10% increase

in average price p er ad (APPA) 11 . Meta reported that these

increases were caused by higher demand from advertisers

and that the demand was caused by their improvements

in ad performance from the implementation of AI. As a

whole, a higher demand for ad space and an increase in

daily activ e people (DAP) led to Meta’s advertising revenue

growing by 21.74% in 2024.

The most important piece of guidance given by Meta was

that they estimate they will spend $60 - $65 billion in

capital expenditures to fuel the advancement of AI. In a

time when the theme of AI is more prevalent than ever,

Meta solidified the direction lar ge tech and

communications companies are going. For our forecast,

we used the high end of the guidance at $65 billion for

capital expenditures in 2025. This figure was referenced

sub_queries: Apple R&D expense and revenue FY2023 FY2024 10-K
Meta R&D expense and revenue FY2023 FY2024 10-K

judgment.reasoning: The response correctly provides Apple's R&D/revenue percentages (7.8% for FY2023, 8.0% for FY2024) and cites sources, but is incomplete: it cuts off before presenting Meta's data and the final comparison. It fails to fully answer the question by not including Meta's figures or the explicit ratio comparison (Meta ~3x more). This matches the M1 factual correctness score of 0.600.
